body_txt: This is when the chances of survival, or the level of opposition, are presented to a character and they're apparently at a disadvantage. They'll respond that they wouldn't have it any other way — often, they'll add a Badass Boast , remarking they've beaten worse odds without breaking a sweat, or that they might give themselves a handicap just for fun . After all, a Million to One Chance always presents an odd of success. Most often said as a Stock Phrase . Variations include when a character initially seems to be complaining about how unfair the odds are, but is actually saying the odds are unfairly stacked in their favor , and when this is said sarcastically . Sometimes comedy will use this for somebody who doesn't really care how poor the odds are and will just go ahead with it anyway despite being told how terrible their chances are. Alternatively, they may not be listening, or they'd actually prefer the failure. Or they might just be too stupid to understand how bad the odds really are . This often works out for these characters due to the Law Of Conservation of Ninjutsu . Sometimes though, odds come through. Compare with Million to One Chance and Audacity Earns Approval . Contrast with its Sister Trope , Never Tell Me the Odds! , which has a similarly badass sentiment but the character is aware the odds stacked against them are dire.
